reasoning: Based on thorough research, the probability that this recall will be closed by July 1, 2025 (87 days after April 5) is approximately 48%. Key factors: 1) While 87 days seems reasonable for a product recall, FSIS procedures require verification of effectiveness checks and proper disposal before closure (search_3_summary). 2) The recall involves ~22,672 lbs distributed across 10 states (search_6_result_3, search_6_result_4), suggesting complex logistics for tracking and recovery. 3) No historical closure timeline data was found, suggesting closures may take longer than expected. 4) The product was produced February 5, 2025 with a sell-by date potentially extending beyond April, meaning some product may still be in consumers' freezers, complicating effectiveness verification. 5) FSIS treats recall verification as priority level 3 (out of 6), which doesn't suggest urgent processing. 6) My initial optimism at 60% was too high given the lack of concrete data supporting rapid closures and the complexity factors identified.
